devanagari: श्रीमद्भागवत महापुराण । षष्ठः स्कन्धःएकोनविंशोऽध्यायः । पुंसवनव्रतविधिःश्रीराजोवाचव्रतं पुंसवनं ब्रह्मन् भवता यदुदीरितम् । तस्य वेदितुमि...
english_devnagari: śrīmadbhāgavata mahāpurāṇa ṣaṣṭhaḥ skandhaḥekonaviṃśo puṃsavanavratavidhiḥśrīrājovācavrataṃ puṃsavanaṃ brahman bhavatā yadudīritam tasya veditumicchāmi yena viṣṇuḥ prasīdati

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The King (Parikṣīt) asked:
I wish to know, Oh Brāhmaṇa Sage, the details of the vow called Puṃsavana which you have mentioned, as Lord Viṣṇu is propitiated thereby.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाचशुक्ले मार्गशिरे पक्षे योषिद्‍भर्तुरनुज्ञया । आरभेत व्रतमिदं सार्वकामिकमादितः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaśukle mārgaśire pakṣe yoṣid‍bharturanujñayā ārabheta vratamidaṃ sārvakāmikamāditaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
With the permission and consent of her husband a woman should begin on the 1st day of the bright half of Mārgaśīrṣa this observance of vow which is calculated to confer all desires on one.

devanagari: निशम्य मरुतां जन्म ब्राह्मणान् अनुमन्त्र्य च । स्नात्वा शुक्लदती शुक्ले वसीतालङ्कृताम्बरे । पूजयेत् प्रातराशात् प्राग् भगवन्तं श्रिया सह...
english_devnagari: niśamya marutāṃ janma brāhmaṇān anumantrya ca snātvā śukladatī śukle vasītālaṅkṛtāmbare pūjayet prātarāśāt prāg bhagavantaṃ śriyā saha
translation: She should listen to the narrative about the birth of Maruts and consult the Brāhmaṇas. Cleansing her teeth white and having taken a bath, she should put on two white garments. Adorning herself with ornaments, she should worship Lord Viṣṇu along with Śrī in the morning, before taking the breakfast.

devanagari: अलं ते निरपेक्षाय पूर्णकाम नमोऽस्तु ते । महाविभूतिपतये नमः सकलसिद्धये ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: alaṃ te nirapekṣāya pūrṇakāma namo mahāvibhūtipataye namaḥ sakalasiddhaye
translation: (She should pray as follows:) “Oh Lord of accomplished desires, I bow to you who, being endowed with enough of all things, are (hence) above wants and expectations. Salutations to you, the Lord of Lakṣmī, the master of all mystic powers.

devanagari: यथा त्वं कृपया भूत्या तेजसा महिमौजसा । जुष्ट ईश गुणैः सर्वैः ततोऽसि भगवान् प्रभुः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā tvaṃ kṛpayā bhūtyā tejasā mahimaujasā juṣṭa īśa guṇaiḥ sarvaiḥ tato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

translation: Oh Lord! Possessor of excellences like supreme grace, fortune, prowess, glory, energy and other divine attributes as you are, you are the real omnipotent Supreme Ruler of the world.

devanagari: विष्णुपत्‍नि महामाये महापुरुषलक्षणे । प्रीयेथा मे महाभागे लोकमातर्नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣṇupat‍ni mahāmāye mahāpuruṣalakṣaṇe prīyethā me mahābhāge lokamātarnamo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative middle second single], [optative passive second single]

translation: Oh Divine consort of Viṣṇu, the supreme deluding power of the Lord, you possess the same characteristics as those of the Supreme Person Viṣṇu. Be propitious unto me, Oh Almighty Goddess. Oh Mother of the universe, I bow to you.

devanagari: ॐ नमो भगवते महापुरुषाय महानुभावाय । महाविभूतिपतये सह महाविभूतिभिः । बलिमुपहरामीति । अनेनाहरहर्मन्त्रेण । विष्णोरावाहनार्घ्यपाद्योपस्पर्श...
english_devnagari: oṃ namo bhagavate mahāpuruṣāya mahānubhāvāya mahāvibhūtipataye saha mahāvibhūtibhiḥ balimupaharāmīti | anenāharaharmantreṇa viṣṇorāvāhanārghyapādyopasparśanasnāna vāsaupavītavibhūṣaṇa gandhapuṣpadhūpadīpopahārādi upacārān susamāhita upāharet
translation:
I offer worship to the glorious Lord designated by the sacred syllable OM, the Lord who is the Supreme Person, most mighty, the Lord of Mahālakṣmī, along with his highest potencies.
Invoking daily god Viṣṇu by chanting the aforesaid mantra, one should offer, with concentrated mind, articles of worship such as water for washing his (Viṣṇu’s) hands and feet, and rinse his (Viṣṇu’s) mouth and water for taking bath, silken garments, the sacred thread, ornaments, sandal-paste, flowers, incense, light, food and other articles.

devanagari: हविःशेषं च जुहुयादनले द्वादशाहुतीः । ॐ नमो भगवते महापुरुषाय महाविभूतिपतये स्वाहेति ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: haviḥśeṣaṃ ca juhuyādanale dvādaśāhutīḥ oṃ namo bhagavate mahāpuruṣāya mahāvibhūtipataye svāheti
translation: Out of the food left after offering it to Viṣṇu, twelve oblations of that food should be offered to the fire with the mantra “Salutation to the glorious Supreme Person (Viṣṇu) of unlimited powers and denoted by the sacred syllable OM. I offer these oblations to you along with Lakṣmī.”

devanagari: श्रियं विष्णुं च वरदावाशिषां प्रभवावुभौ । भक्त्या सम्पूजयेन्नित्यं यदीच्छेत्सर्वसम्पदः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: śriyaṃ viṣṇuṃ ca varadāvāśiṣāṃ prabhavāvubhau bhaktyā sampūjayennityaṃ yadīcchetsarvasampadaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: If one is desirous of securing all worldly prosperity, one should always devoutly worship goddess Lakṣmī and Lord Viṣṇu who confer boons and both of whom are the fountainheads of all blessings.

devanagari: प्रणमेद् दण्डवद्‍भूमौ भक्तिप्रह्वेण चेतसा । दशवारं जपेन्मन्त्रं ततः स्तोत्रमुदीरयेत् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: praṇamed daṇḍavad‍bhūmau bhaktiprahveṇa cetasā daśavāraṃ japenmantraṃ tataḥ stotramudīrayet
translation: She should prostrate herself before them just like a rod on the ground, with her heart submissive in devotion. She should repeat the (above-mentioned) mantra for ten times and sing (the following) hymn:

devanagari: युवां तु विश्वस्य विभू जगतः कारणं परम् । इयं हि प्रकृतिः सूक्ष्मा मायाशक्तिर्दुरत्यया ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: yuvāṃ tu viśvasya vibhū jagataḥ kāraṇaṃ param iyaṃ hi prakṛtiḥ sūkṣmā māyāśaktirduratyayā
translation: “Both of you are the Lords of the universe, the ultimate cause of the world. This is verily the subtle Prakṛti, your deluding potency (Māyā) too formidable to be overcome.

devanagari: तस्या अधीश्वरः साक्षात् त्वमेव पुरुषः परः । त्वं सर्वयज्ञ इज्येयं क्रियेयं फलभुग्भवान् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyā adhīśvaraḥ sākṣāt tvameva puruṣaḥ paraḥ tvaṃ sarvayajña ijyeyaṃ kriyeyaṃ phalabhugbhavān
translation: You are directly her Supreme Ruler, the Supreme Person. You are the embodiment of all sacrifices while she is the sacrificial performance. She is activity itself while you are the enjoyer of the fruit thereof.

devanagari: गुणव्यक्तिरियं देवी व्यञ्जको गुणभुग्भवान् । त्वं हि सर्वशरीर्यात्मा श्रीः शरीरेन्द्रियाशयाः । नामरूपे भगवती प्रत्ययस्त्वं अपाश्रयः ॥ १३...
english_devnagari: guṇavyaktiriyaṃ devī vyañjako guṇabhugbhavān tvaṃ hi sarvaśarīryātmā śrīḥ śarīrendriyāśayāḥ nāmarūpe bhagavatī pratyayastvaṃ apāśrayaḥ
translation: This goddess is the manifestation of guṇas, while you are the cause of that manifestation and the enjoyer of the guṇas. You are the Soul, the inner controller of all embodied beings, while Śrī is the body, senses and the inner organ. The glorious Lakṣmī represents the name and form, while you are their support and their illuminator.

devanagari: यथा युवां त्रिलोकस्य वरदौ परमेष्ठिनौ । तथा म उत्तमश्लोक सन्तु सत्या महाशिषः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā yuvāṃ trilokasya varadau parameṣṭhinau tathā ma uttamaśloka santu satyā mahāśiṣaḥ
translation: Inasmuch as both of you Supreme Rulers confer boons on the three worlds, Oh Lord of hallowing renown, may my great desires be fulfilled.”

devanagari: इत्यभिष्टूय वरदं श्रीनिवासं श्रिया सह । तन्निःसार्योपहरणं दत्त्वाऽचमनमर्चयेत् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: ityabhiṣṭūya varadaṃ śrīnivāsaṃ śriyā saha tanniḥsāryopaharaṇaṃ dattvā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ sṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sṛ class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ sṛ ]
[vocative single from √ sṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sṛ class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ sṛ ]
[nominative single from √ sṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ sṛ class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ sṛ ]

translation: Eulogizing thus the Lord of Śrī along with Śrī who confers boons, one should remove all articles used in worship. Having offered water to rinse the mouth (to the deities), one should continue to worship, (known as uttara-pūjā)

devanagari: ततः स्तुवीत स्तोत्रेण भक्तिप्रह्वेण चेतसा । यज्ञोच्छिष्टमवघ्राय पुनरभ्यर्चयेद् हरिम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ stuvīta stotreṇa bhaktiprahveṇa cetasā yajñocchiṣṭamavaghrāya punarabhyarcayed harim
translation: She should then praise with the above hymn, with her mind merged in devotion, Smelling the remnants of the offerings to fire, she should again worship Hari.

devanagari: पतिं च परया भक्त्या महापुरुषचेतसा । प्रियैस्तैस्तैरुपनमेत् प्रेमशीलः स्वयं पतिः । बिभृयात् सर्वकर्माणि पत्‍न्या उच्चावचानि च ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: patiṃ ca parayā bhaktyā mahāpuruṣacetasā priyaistaistairupanamet premaśīlaḥ svayaṃ patiḥ bibhṛyāt sarvakarmāṇi pat‍nyā uccāvacāni ca
translation: Identifying with great devotion her husband with Lord Viṣṇu, she should serve him with various objects dear to him. The husband who is loving by nature, should himself help in all the activities of his wife, whether they be great or small, pertaining to the worship of God.

devanagari: कृतमेकतरेणापि दम्पत्योरुभयोरपि । पत्‍न्यां कुर्यादनर्हायां पतिरेतत् समाहितः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛtamekatareṇāpi dampatyorubhayorapi pat‍nyāṃ kuryādanarhāyāṃ patiretat samāhitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: It is enough if this vow is observed by one of the couple (husband and wife). If the wife is incapable (for reasons of health) or disqualified (due to monthly course), the husband should observe it with concentrated mind.

devanagari: विष्णोर्व्रतमिदं बिभ्रन् न विहन्यात् कथञ्चन । विप्रान् स्त्रियो वीरवतीः स्रग्गन्धबलिमण्डनैः । अर्चेदहरहर्भक्त्या देवं नियममास्थितः ॥ १९...
english_devnagari: viṣṇorvratamidaṃ bibhran na vihanyāt kathañcana viprān striyo vīravatīḥ sraggandhabalimaṇḍanaiḥ arcedaharaharbhaktyā devaṃ niyamamāsthitaḥ
translation: This vow pertaining to Viṣṇu, if once undertaken, should not be violated under any circumstances. Every day she should worship with devotion Brāhmaṇas and women whose husbands are alive, by offering them wreaths of flowers, sandal-paste, food and ornaments, and should also worship Lord Viṣṇu.

devanagari: उद्वास्य देवं स्वे धाम्नि तन्निवेदितमग्रतः । अद्यात् आत्मविशुद्ध्यर्थं सर्वकामर्द्धये तथा ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: udvāsya devaṃ sve dhāmni tanniveditamagrataḥ adyāt ātmaviśuddhyarthaṃ sarvakāmarddhaye tathā
translation: After requesting the deity to retire to his abode, one should eat what was offered to the deity, for self-purification and attainment of all desires.

devanagari: एतेन पूजाविधिना मासान् द्वादश हायनम् । नीत्वाथोपचरेत् साध्वी कार्तिके चरमेऽहनि ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: etena pūjāvidhinā māsān dvādaśa hāyanam nītvāthopacaret sādhvī kārtike carame
translation: After performing such process of worship for a period of twelve months, the pious lady should observe fast on the last day of the month of Kārttika.

devanagari: श्वोभूतेऽप उपस्पृश्य कृष्णमभ्यर्च्य पूर्ववत् । पयःश्रृतेन जुहुयात् चरुणा सह सर्पिषा । पाकयज्ञविधानेन द्वादशैवाहुतीः पतिः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: śvobhūte payaḥśrṛtena juhuyāt caruṇā saha sarpiṣā pākayajñavidhānena dvādaśaivāhutīḥ patiḥ
translation: When the morrow dawns, she should touch water (take bath) and duly worship Lord Kṛṣṇa as before. The husband should offer twelve oblations according to pārvaṇa-sthālīpāka vidhāna (a kind of sacrificial performance) of rice cooked in milk and mixed with ghee, (in the sacrificial fire).[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
The text of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa used by Bhāgavata Candrikā adds:
“Looking upon Brāhmaṇas as the embodiments or forms of the Supreme Soul, she should serve meals to them with devotion and should give to them sesamum seeds coated with jaggery and fried grains, and drinking vessels full of water.”

devanagari: आशिषः शिरसाऽदाय द्विजैः प्रीतैः समीरिताः । प्रणम्य शिरसा भक्त्या भुञ्जीत तदनुज्ञया ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: āśiṣaḥ śirasā praṇamya śirasā bhaktyā bhuñjīta tadanujñayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental plural from √ prī class 4 verb], [instrumental plural from √ prī class 9 verb]
[instrumental plural from √ prī class 4 verb], [instrumental plural from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation: Having reverentially accepted the blessings pronounced by the Brāhmaṇas who are pleased, and having devoutly paid obeisance to them by bowing the head, she should take meals with their permission.

devanagari: आचार्यमग्रतः कृत्वा वाग्यतः सह बन्धुभिः । दद्यात्पत्‍न्यै चरोः शेषं सुप्रजास्त्वं सुसौभगम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: ācāryamagrataḥ kṛtvā vāgyataḥ saha bandhubhiḥ dadyātpat‍nyai caroḥ śeṣaṃ suprajāstvaṃ susaubhagam
translation: After feeding the preceptor first along with the relatives, the husband, with his speech restrained, should give the remnants of the caru, which is capable of conferring good progeny and ensuring good fortune, to his wife.

devanagari: एतच्चरित्वा विधिवद्व्रतं विभोः । अभीप्सितार्थं लभते पुमानिह । स्त्री त्वेतदास्थाय लभेत सौभगं । श्रियं प्रजां जीवपतिं यशो गृहम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: etaccaritvā vidhivadvrataṃ vibhoḥ abhīpsitārthaṃ labhate pumāniha strī tvetadāsthāya labheta saubhagaṃ śriyaṃ prajāṃ jīvapatiṃ yaśo gṛham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: A man who observes this vow of the Almighty God with due formalities, gets his desired object. A woman who observes this will get good fortune, wealth, progeny, longevity to her husband (throughout her life), glory and a good house.

devanagari: कन्या च विन्देत समग्रलक्षणं । वरं त्ववीरा हतकिल्बिषां गतिम् । मृतप्रजा जीवसुता धनेश्वरी । सुदुर्भगा सुभगा रूपमग्र्यम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: kanyā ca vindeta samagralakṣaṇaṃ varaṃ tvavīrā hatakilbiṣāṃ gatim mṛtaprajā jīvasutā dhaneśvarī sudurbhagā subhagā rūpamagryam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: An unmarried girl will get a husband possessing all good characteristics. A widow will be rid of her sins and attain to Liberation. A woman who loses her children in death) will get long-lived children. A woman if ill-fated in spite of wealthy condition, becomes blessed with good fortune and if ugly looking, becomes beautiful. A chronic patient of long period gets free from disease, and becomes energetic and virile in his senses and activities. If it is repeated at the time of auspicious ceremonies, it gives eternal and infinite satisfaction to forefathers and divinities.

devanagari: विन्देद् विरूपा विरुजा विमुच्यते । य आमयावीन्द्रियकल्यदेहम् । एतत्पठन्नभ्युदये च कर्मणि । अनन्ततृप्तिः पितृदेवतानाम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: vinded virūpā virujā vimucyate ya āmayāvīndriyakalyadeham etatpaṭhannabhyudaye ca karmaṇi anantatṛptiḥ pitṛdevatānām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ paṭh class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ paṭh class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: An unmarried girl will get a husband possessing all good characteristics. A widow will be rid of her sins and attain to Liberation. A woman who loses her children in death) will get long-lived children. A woman if ill-fated in spite of wealthy condition, becomes blessed with good fortune and if ugly looking, becomes beautiful. A chronic patient of long period gets free from disease, and becomes energetic and virile in his senses and activities. If it is repeated at the time of auspicious ceremonies, it gives eternal and infinite satisfaction to forefathers and divinities.

devanagari: तुष्टाः प्रयच्छन्ति समस्तकामान् । होमावसाने हुतभुक् श्रीः हरिश्च । राजन् महन्मरुतां जन्म पुण्यं । दितेर्व्रतं चाभिहितं महत्ते ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tuṣṭāḥ prayacchanti samastakāmān homāvasāne hutabhuk śrīḥ hariśca rājan mahanmarutāṃ janma puṇyaṃ ditervrataṃ cābhihitaṃ mahatte

translation:
If the Fire-God, the goddess Lakṣmī and Lord Viṣṇu are pleased after the completion of Homa (sacrifice), they confer all desired objects. Oh King, I have narrated to you the auspicious story of the birth of Maruts and the great vow observed by Diti.
